---

### 2026.4

This release introduces a major change to how CSS classes work in Tailwind projects,
adds a revamped sticky header, enhances the AI agent chat with a new streaming/thinking UI,
and includes a number of security, bug, and dependency fixes.

### Native Tailwind / DaisyUI Classes by Default

Previously, Pegasus projects used its own set of `pg-` prefixed CSS classes
(e.g., `pg-button-primary`, `pg-title`) that mapped to the underlying framework's
styles via Sass `@extend` or Tailwind `@apply`.

**New Tailwind projects now output native Tailwind and DaisyUI classes directly**
(e.g., `btn btn-primary`, `text-3xl font-bold mb-2`).
This means you can style your app using the standard
[Tailwind](https://tailwindcss.com/) and [DaisyUI](https://daisyui.com/) documentation,
without needing to learn or look up Pegasus-specific class names.

**Existing projects can stay on `pg-` classes** by checking the "Use Pegasus CSS classes"
option in your project configuration — nothing will change for you until you decide to migrate.

**Bootstrap and Bulma projects are unaffected** and continue to use `pg-` classes as before.

A migration script (`scripts/migrate_pg_css_classes.py`) is included in new projects
to help existing Tailwind projects migrate from `pg-` classes to native classes when ready.
It supports `--dry-run` so you can preview changes before applying them.

See [the updated CSS docs](/css/pegasus-css/) for full details, including which classes
are covered by the migration and which (like chat and breadcrumb styles) remain as `pg-`.

### Improved AI Agent Chat Experience

The agent streaming system has been refactored to use typed events
(`ThinkingTextEvent`, `ToolCallEvent`, `ToolResultEvent`, `FinalTextEvent`) powered
by `pydantic_ai`'s `agent.iter()`. This enables a richer chat experience:

- **Added a collapsible "thinking" section** in the chat UI that shows the agent's
  reasoning and tool calls as they happen.
- **The chat overlay is now resizable** — drag the left edge to change the width,
  and the size is remembered across sessions.
- Added tests for the new agent streaming behavior.

### Supply Chain Protections

A few changes to help protect against supply-chain attacks on package dependencies:

- **Added a 7-day cooldown before picking up newly-published packages**, so that compromised
  packages caught shortly after release don't end up in your project. This is applied via
  `uv`'s `exclude-newer` setting for Python and npm's `min-release-age` setting for JavaScript.
  The `upgrade-python-deps` Claude Code skill has been updated to pass the same flag.
- **Added a committed `package-lock.json`** (and `frontend/package-lock.json` for standalone
  frontend builds) to generated projects. This ensures `npm install` produces deterministic
  results out of the box and gives you a lockfile to review when dependencies change.

### Other Changes

- **Overhauled the default top navigation** with a sticky header that uses a glassmorphism
  effect, plus a new mobile slide-in drawer for smaller screens.
- **Upgraded Node and npm to their latest versions**, and upgraded most JavaScript packages.
- **Upgraded TypeScript to version 6**, with associated `tsconfig.json` fixes.
- Overhauled logging to be more consistent.
  - Use `__name__` everywhere in loggers instead of custom names.
  - Always name logger variables `logger`.
- Added validation to Stripe price IDs — prices are only sent to Stripe when they match
  the expected format. Also added tests for this.
- Ran an AI-assisted security and bug audit across the codebase, which surfaced only
  minor issues. Fixes included:
  - Catching `IntegrityError` in the ecommerce purchase flow to avoid a race
    between the Stripe webhook and the success redirect.
  - Gating the admin AI agent WebSocket to superusers only.
  - Making chat `message_type` read-only on the API so clients can't inject system-role messages.
  - HTML-escaping streamed AI chat tokens to prevent XSS via model output.
  - Fixing `subscription_is_trialing` to use `convert_tstamp` instead of comparing
    a unix timestamp to a datetime.
  - Including trialing subscriptions when syncing seat quantities.
  - Catching invalid user timezones in the locale middleware instead of crashing.
  - Added a broad set of new tests around the above areas, including timezone middleware
    handling, teams middleware 404 behavior, and the chat message API (covering auth,
    cross-user access, and system-message injection attempts).
- The team context processor now falls back to `default_team` when no team is set in the URL.
